diff:
diff --git a/.dockerignore b/.dockerignore
index 6b8710a..2ca81b8 100644
--- a/.dockerignore
+++ b/.dockerignore
@@ -1 +1,2 @@
 .git
+test_files/webdav-tls/
diff --git a/docker-compose.yml b/docker-compose.yml
index 9de0d87..c5b8d8d 100644
--- a/docker-compose.yml
+++ b/docker-compose.yml
@@ -23,6 +23,17 @@ services:
       USERNAME: alice
       PASSWORD: secret1234
       ANONYMOUS_METHODS: 'GET,OPTIONS'
+  webdav-tls:
+    image: nginx:alpine
+    restart: always
+    ports:
+      - "4443:443"
+    volumes:
+      - ./test_files/webdav-tls/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf:ro
+      - ./test_files/webdav-tls/cert.pem:/etc/nginx/ssl/cert.pem:ro
+      - ./test_files/webdav-tls/key.pem:/etc/nginx/ssl/key.pem:ro
+    depends_on:
+      - webdav
diff --git a/phpunit.xml.dist b/phpunit.xml.dist
index 65347bf..b6ba7d9 100644
--- a/phpunit.xml.dist
+++ b/phpunit.xml.dist
@@ -7,6 +7,8 @@
     </testsuites>
     <php>
         <env name="FLYSYSTEM_TEST_SFTP" value="yes" />
+        <env name="FLYSYSTEM_TEST_WEBDAV_TLS_HOST" value="localhost" />
+        <env name="FLYSYSTEM_TEST_WEBDAV_TLS_PORT" value="4443" />
     </php>

exploration_log.aha_moment: The existing webdav service on port 4080 is the upstream target. The TLS proxy just needs to reference it by service name in nginx, and the test env vars match the exposed host port 4443.
exploration_log.hindsight: Nothing to change; all three file changes were clear from the task description.

alternatives: Use a different TLS proxy (haproxy, traefik)
key_decisions.decision: Use nginx:alpine as specified in the task
Use port 4443 to avoid port conflicts and follow the existing port scheme (4040, 4080)
key_decisions.reasoning: Pattern established by existing WebDAV ports

queries.query_text: Fix docker-compose.yml to add nginx WebDAV TLS proxy service and update phpunit.xml.dist with FLYSYSTEM_TEST_WEBDAV_TLS_HOST and FLYSYSTEM_TEST_WEBDAV_TLS_PORT

solve_notes: Added an `nginx:alpine` `webdav-tls` service to `docker-compose.yml` that exposes port 4443 and proxies HTTPS to the existing `webdav` service via mounted nginx config and self-signed certificate files from `test_files/webdav-tls/`. Added `FLYSYSTEM_TEST_WEBDAV_TLS_HOST` and `FLYSYSTEM_TEST_WEBDAV_TLS_PORT` env vars to `phpunit.xml.dist`, and added `test_files/webdav-tls/` to `.dockerignore`.
task_text:
Fix `docker-compose.yml` WebDAV service not exposing TLS port for HTTPS testing

The `docker-compose.yml` file configures a `sabredav` service on port
4040 and a `bytemark/webdav` service on port 4080, both over plain
HTTP. The `WebDAV/WebDAVAdapter` supports HTTPS connections, but
there is no TLS-enabled service for integration testing. Add a TLS
proxy service to `docker-compose.yml` using `nginx:alpine` with a
self-signed certificate that proxies to the existing WebDAV service.
Update `phpunit.xml.dist` to add environment variables
`FLYSYSTEM_TEST_WEBDAV_TLS_HOST` and `FLYSYSTEM_TEST_WEBDAV_TLS_PORT`
for the new service, and update `.dockerignore` to exclude test
certificates from non-test Docker contexts.

test_selection.coverage_skip_reason: Auditor could not generate baseline coverage — no coverage report file found in repository root

thrash_preventing_defs.name: sabredav
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: seeing both WebDAV services (sabredav and webdav) prevents the agent from attaching the TLS proxy to the wrong one and clarifies which ports are already in use

thrash_preventing_defs.name: FLYSYSTEM_TEST_SFTP
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: seeing the existing env var format prevents the agent from using a different XML element or attribute format

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers the directly edited items. An AI agent would additionally read the sabredav service to understand all existing port allocations and avoid conflicts, and read the FLYSYSTEM_TEST_SFTP env var to understand the existing env var XML format before adding new ones.
